Title: Involvement of sweet pepper CaLOX2 in jasmonate-dependent induced defence against Western flower thrips

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipoxygenase family; PLAT/LH2 domain; LOX domain; 13-type LOX
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids in the 13-LOX branch to initiate octadecanoid pathway flux leading to OPDA, JA and JA-Ile biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported for herbivory-induced jasmonate accumulation; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Induced defense against Western flower thrips; reduces thrips feeding, preference, and population development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes herbivory-induced accumulation of OPDA, JA and JA-Ile and enhances resistance to thrips
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in leaves upon thrips feeding at 5 h, 10 h and 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in sweet pepper leaves upon thrips feeding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Western flower thrips feeding; exogenous jasmonic acid treatment; VIGS silencing context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; synteny analysis; RT-qPCR under thrips feeding; VIGS knockdown; HPLC-MS/MS phytohormone quantification; thrips no-choice and choice bioassays; exogenous JA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS construct for silencing; specific promoter not reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CaLOX2 silencing increases susceptibility to thrips; broader effects on other JA-dependent defenses or secondary metabolites are suggested but not directly measured
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the closest pepper homolog of tomato SlLOXD; phylogenetically grouped with Solanaceae LOX proteins involved in JA biosynthesis; conserved synteny on chromosome 3 with tomato SlLOXD and potato StLOXH3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaLOX2 was identified as a pepper 13-type lipoxygenase orthologous to tomato SlLOXD and associated with the octadecanoid pathway. 2. Thrips feeding induced CaLOX2 transcript accumulation in leaves. 3. VIGS of CaLOX2 reduced herbivory-induced OPDA, JA and JA-Ile accumulation. 4. CaLOX2 silencing increased thrips feeding damage and host preference, demonstrating reduced defense. 5. Exogenous JA phenocopied enhanced defense, supporting CaLOX2 function through jasmonate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CaLOX2 is a candidate target to enhance endogenous jasmonate-mediated insect resistance in pepper; useful for marker-assisted selection or expression engineering, but constitutive activation of JA signaling should be balanced against possible defense-growth tradeoffs not tested in this study.
